source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff to the CEO
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HOMEVISION

HomeVision is building the next generation of AI-powered software for the $13 trillion mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA, automates the manual, costly, and error-prone review of documents. By automating these tasks, we help lenders close loans faster and with less friction—ultimately saving borrowers time, money, and stress. We have already transformed appraisal operations for the country's top lenders, cutting review times by over 75%, and are now aggressively expanding to cover the entire loan file. We are a well-funded startup backed by Initialized Capital and NVP Capital, looking for builders who want to solve hard problems.




THE ROLE & CULTURE

We are seeking a sharp, organized Chief of Staff to support HomeVision’s CEO and company operations. This role is focused on acting as a strategic operational partner to the CEO, facilitating cohesion between strategic priorities, competitive positioning, and cross-functional operations, and supporting fundraising efforts . You will manage synthesis of company performance to allow our CEO to focus on building the company, gaining exposure to every part of the business in the process. The Chief of Staff will have a front seat to decision making processes across finance, org development, partnerships, product and more. In this role, you will learn to bridge the gap between P&L management and long term strategic decision making.

At HomeVision, we like working with smart, curious, and kind people who enjoy real responsibility. We value low ego, high ownership, and clear communication. We learn from one another, give direct feedback, and and prioritize doing right by each other and our customers. This role is a good fit if you have high agency, excellent judgment, and thrive in ambiguity.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Strategic Coordination: Coordinate content for and attend executive team meetings, track progress, and serve as a liaison between the CEO and other departments. Surface most important weekly & monthly insights, blockers, and priorities to CEO.

 - Communications: Draft internal communications and presentations, distilling complex information into clear, concise formats.

 - Corporate Development:
   
   - Board & Investor Relations: Prepare materials for board meetings and investor communications. Lead fundraising material preparation and data room.
   
   - Fundraising: Partner with the CEO to translate the company model and positioning into a cohesive and compelling fundraising narrative. Provide input to bridge the gap between the operating model and longer term projections.
   
   - Strategic Partnerships: Partnerships: Evaluate potential partnerships and revenue share agreements with an eye on overall value delivered to customers. Work with finance and operations to maintain margin analysis to support partnership building. Travel to conferences and customer on-sites to gain industry context.

 - AI & Automation: Use AI tools to streamline & automate G&A workflows, to support strategic coordination among the exec team.


WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Experience: 4-6 years of experience in consulting, finance, operations, or a high-growth startup environment. Prior fundraising, M&A, or partnerships experience a plus.

 - Education: Bachelor's degree in economics, business, engineering, or a related field. MBA from top program a plus, but not required.

 - AI Fluency: Highly fluent with AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT) for research, analysis, writing, and automation.

 - Communication: Strong written communication skills with the ability to distill complex information into clear, concise formats.

 - Organization: Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail.

 - Mindset: High agency and judgment—you know when to escalate and when to solve problems independently. Comfortable working across all levels of an organization.


COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary based on experience and meaningful equity package dependent on experience.

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer, AI & Data Security (Contract, Argentina)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HOMEVISION

HomeVision is building the next generation of AI-powered software for the $13 trillion mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA (Machine Intelligence Review Assistant), automates labor-intensive document processing and underwriting, helping lenders close loans faster and with less friction — ultimately saving borrowers time, money, and stress. We have already transformed appraisal operations for the country's top lenders, cutting review times by over 75%. Now, we are aggressively expanding MIRA to cover the entire loan file — automating income, assets, and credit analysis — to create a holistic underwriting utility for the industry.



We look for hungry, smart, curious and kind people who enjoy hard problems and real responsibility. Engineers own meaningful projects end to end, from design to deployment, and collaborate closely with product, design and our go-to-market teams. We value low ego, high ownership, clear communication, and respect for each other's time. We always try to "do the loving thing" in how we work together and with our customers and partners.



THE ROLE

This is a focused infrastructure role at the intersection of AI and data security. As we expand MIRA across the entire loan file, our hardest challenges shift from model building to the infrastructure needed to securely develop, test and run AI systems. You'll drive that infrastructure. It's a backend engineering role where you will work in application code as well as in Terraform/AWS with a security-first mindset.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Plan, design, and build

 - Secure infrastructure for AI-assisted development and testing

 - Data-access security across the platform

 - Cross-cutting application features

You will manage your own projects, communicating designs, plans, and status updates proactively. We work in Go, Python, and TypeScript with a focus on reliability and observability.



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

• Strong backend engineering experience

• A security-first mindset: you naturally consider data access and potential leak vectors for every process, person, and agent.

• Genuine interest in AI/LLM systems and the infrastructure that makes them safe and reliable to build on

• The ability to take an ambiguous, hard problem from design doc to production

• Strong verbal and written communication in English



BONUS POINTS

• Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform) and observability tooling (e.g. Datadog)

• Experience designing infrastructure on AWS — isolation/sandboxing, networking, and the primitives that keep systems secure under real load

• Residence in Rosario or Buenos Aires (Residence in Argentina is mandatory)



WHAT WE'LL OFFER YOU

• A competitive salary in USD

• A high degree of autonomy and ownership of your work

• Support for your professional goals and learning within and beyond the company, through your manager and peers

NOTES
Recruiter and AI submissions will be rejected.
All hires will be subject to thorough criminal, education, work history, and reference checks.
Compensation is DOE.

source_jobs.title: Staff Front End Engineer - AI Product UX - US - San Francisco (Hybrid)
source_jobs.description:
STAFF FRONTEND ENGINEER, AI PRODUCT UX

Location: San Francisco - hybrid, in-office two days a week

Compensation: Competitive base + leadership-level equity


HELP DEFINE HOW HUMANS TRUST AI DECISIONS

AI is transforming mortgage lending, but underwriting decisions still require humans to verify, interpret, question, and approve what AI systems produce. The challenge is not just delivering automation. It is designing interfaces that help professionals confidently work alongside AI in high-stakes financial workflows.


WHO WE ARE

HomeVision is the AI underwriting platform for the $13 trillion U.S. mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA, has already automated more than two million appraisal reviews for many of the country’s largest lenders and appraisal management companies, reducing review time by 75% and doubling underwriter productivity.

Now we’re expanding beyond appraisal review to cover the full underwriting workflow - income, assets, credit, collateral, and loan file decisioning - to create the first end-to-end AI underwriting platform for the industry. We have contracted production demand from real enterprise customers, including Newrez, the fourth-largest U.S. mortgage lender and a strategic investor, alongside our backers Initialized Capital and NVP Capital..

We’re looking for a staff/principal-level frontend/product engineer to help define the interaction layer between humans and AI across the underwriting process. You’ll partner directly with our Head of Design, our CTO, and senior product leadership to shape the platform’s core interaction model, not just implement it.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Define the future of AI-assisted underwriting. Design and build workflows that help underwriters, processors, and loan officers confidently work alongside AI systems and build trust. Create intuitive UX patterns for explainability, auditability, exception handling, and human-in-the-loop review.

 - Own the cross-platform frontend and design system. Lead the evolution of our frontend platform across web and future embedded experiences, including in-document review and lender integrations. Architect components so the team can ship faster as we scale.

 - Elevate the team. Establish the frontend patterns, libraries, and review standards the rest of the engineering team builds against. Mentor and recruit the engineers who’ll work alongside you.

 - Ship fast and learn faster. Take features from rough ideas to production in days, not weeks. Watch how customers actually use them and rapidly iterate.

 - Drive tools and process improvement. Share ideas and test tools that accelerate prototyping to production at higher velocity and quality.


WHO YOU ARE

 - You’re deeply product-oriented. You enjoy engaging with customers and learning a new domain. You think beyond implementation details and care how technical decisions shape user behavior and product outcomes.

 - You’re a builder at heart. You’ve shipped meaningful products used by real customers. You likely have side projects, strong opinions about technology, and genuine curiosity about where AI is heading. You are a creator.

 - You operate with urgency and ownership. You move quickly without sacrificing reliability or trust. You proactively identify problems, communicate clearly, and drive solutions.

 - You elevate the people around you. You bring strong engineering judgment, high standards, and collaborative energy. Teams become stronger when you’re involved.

 - You’re excited by difficult workflow problems. Complex systems, making sense of dense information and documents, operational tooling, and AI-assisted decision-making genuinely interest you.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING ON THE TECHNICAL SIDE

 - Deep proficiency in React and TypeScript is ideal but not required. We trust that great engineers can come up to speed quickly.

 - A clear sense of how to architect front-end systems that can extend and scale.

 - Comfort working across the stack when it matters - you can read and reas

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Contract - Argentina
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HOMEVISION:

HomeVision is building the next generation of AI-powered software for the $13 trillion mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA (Machine Intelligence Review Assistant), automates labor-intensive document processing and underwriting, helping lenders close loans faster and with less friction - ultimately saving borrowers time, money, and stress. We have already transformed appraisal operations for the country's top lenders, cutting review times by over 75%. Now, we are aggressively expanding MIRA to cover the entire loan file - automating income, assets, and credit analysis - to create a holistic underwriting utility for the industry.

At HomeVision, we like working with hungry, smart, curious and kind people who enjoy hard problems and real responsibility. Engineers here own meaningful projects end to end, from design to deployment, and collaborate closely with product, design and our go-to-market teams. We value low ego, high ownership, clear communication, and respect for each other’s time. We always try to “do the loving thing” in how we work together and with our customers and partners.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

This role is unique because it requires the ability to contribute directly to our codebase, extend and architect our platform with an eye towards growth, and mentor other engineers. The ideal candidate will have deep technical experience to set a broad technical vision, initiate its development, and strong leadership skills to unify a team behind its execution.

We build rapidly using React/Typescript, Golang, and Python on AWS. Our engineers work on entire products— everything from workflow engines, NLP and image processing, to building frontend data visualizations that help communicate results in an intuitive way.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING:

 - Strong capabilities in full-stack development with deep backend experience

 - Ability to learn and master new programming languages and technologies

 - Experience mentoring engineers, running a development process, and building a culture that emphasizes responsibility, rigor, and collaboration

 - Strong verbal and written communication in English


BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience in scientific and numerical computing (e.g., numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, tensorflow, pytorch, jupyter) considered a plus

 - Experience in one or more of the following technologies: Go, Python, React/Typescript, Elasticsearch, and Docker and familiarity with machine learning concepts.

 - Residence in Rosario or Buenos Aires (Residence in Argentina is mandatory)


WHAT WE'LL OFFER YOU

 - A competitive salary in USD

 - A high degree of autonomy and ownership of your work

 - Support for your professional goals and learning within and beyond the company, through your manager and peers

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer, Agentic Productivity and Infrastructure - San Francisco
source_jobs.description:
STAFF ENGINEER, AGENTIC PRODUCTIVITY & INFRASTRUCTURE

Location: San Francisco - hybrid, in-office two days a week

Compensation: Competitive base + leadership-level equity


MAKE OUR ENGINEERING ORG AS AGENTIC AS OUR PRODUCT

AI is transforming mortgage lending - and it’s transforming how software gets built. We build AI agents for our customers; we should be just as aggressive about using agents to build and operate HomeVision itself. The opportunity is to make our own software development lifecycle and platform operations radically more automated and agentic, so our small team can ship and operate far more than our headcount would suggest.


WHO WE ARE

HomeVision is the AI underwriting platform for the $13 trillion U.S. mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA, has already automated more than two million appraisal reviews for many of the country’s largest lenders and appraisal management companies, reducing review time by 75% and doubling underwriter productivity.

Now we’re expanding beyond appraisal review to cover the full underwriting workflow - income, assets, credit, collateral, and loan file decisioning - to create the first end-to-end AI underwriting platform for the industry. MIRA’s been adopted by dozens of real enterprise customers with contracted production demand, including Newrez, the fourth-largest mortgage lender in the U.S. and a strategic investor in HomeVision, alongside our venture backers, Initialized Capital and NVP Capital.

We’re looking for a staff-level engineer to own internal infrastructure and agentic productivity - the tools, systems, and agents that make our SDLC and platform maintenance faster, safer, and more automated. This is a force-multiplier role for the entire engineering org. You’ll partner directly with our CTO and engineering leadership to decide where agents and automation create the most leverage, and then build it.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Streamline the build-and-ship loop. Design internal systems and agents that accelerate how we write, review, test, and ship code - turning manual engineering toil into automated, agent-assisted workflows.

 - Automate platform operations. Build the systems that keep the platform healthy with less human effort - CI/CD, observability, alerting, and agent-assisted monitoring and incident response.

 - Continually improve our internal developer platform. Create the paved roads, tooling, and environments that let every engineer ship faster and more safely, and that make our agentic workflows reliable and repeatable.

 - Seek leverage. Identify where automation and agents pay off most across the engineering org, prototype quickly, and measure the impact on velocity and reliability.

 - Elevate the team. Establish the internal tooling patterns, libraries, and standards the rest of the team builds against. Mentor and recruit the engineers who’ll work alongside you.


WHO YOU ARE

 - You’re deeply customer-focused. But in this case, your “customers” are your engineering teammates. You care about their workflows and the outcomes your tools produce, and you measure success by how much faster and safer the org ships.

 - You’re a builder at heart. You’ve shipped meaningful systems used by real people. You likely have side projects, strong opinions about technology, and genuine curiosity about where AI is heading. You are a creator.

 - You operate with urgency and ownership. You move quickly without sacrificing reliability or trust. You proactively identify problems, communicate clearly, and drive solutions.

 - You elevate the people around you. You bring strong engineering judgment, high standards, and collaborative energy. Teams become stronger when you’re involved.

 - You’re excited by difficult systems problems. Developer experience, automation, agent reliability, observability, and AI-assisted operations genuinely interest you.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING ON THE TECHNICAL SIDE

 - Strong proficiency in Go, Python, and/or TypeScript is a bonus, but we trust that g

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer, Agentic Intelligence - San Francisco
source_jobs.description:
STAFF ENGINEER, AGENTIC INTELLIGENCE

Location: San Francisco - hybrid, in-office two days a week

Compensation: Competitive base + leadership-level equity


BUILD THE POLICY FRAMEWORK THAT MAKES AN UNDERWRITING AGENT TRUSTWORTHY

Mortgage underwriting is the work of deciding whether a loan can be approved - checking a borrower’s income, assets, credit, and collateral against a dense web of rules and documents. We’re teaching an AI agent to do that work. But an agent is only as good as the policies that govern it. The hard part isn’t getting a model to produce an answer - it’s encoding underwriting judgment into the policies the agent reasons against, and building a framework flexible enough that those policies can be configured, customized, and changed as fast as the business and its lenders demand.

That’s the problem we’re solving.


WHO WE ARE

HomeVision is the AI underwriting platform for the $13 trillion U.S. mortgage industry. Our platform, MIRA, has already automated more than two million appraisal reviews for many of the country’s largest lenders and appraisal management companies, reducing review time by 75% and doubling underwriter productivity.

Now we’re expanding beyond appraisal review to cover the full underwriting workflow - income, assets, credit, collateral, and loan file decisioning - to create the first end-to-end AI underwriting platform for the industry. MIRA’s been adopted by dozens of real enterprise customers with contracted production demand, including Newrez, the fourth-largest mortgage lender in the U.S. and a strategic investor in HomeVision, alongside our venture backers, Initialized Capital and NVP Capital.

We’re looking for a staff-level engineer to own the intelligence and policy layer of our underwriting agent - the policies that encode underwriting judgment and the configurable framework that lets us author, customize, and dynamically change them across lenders, investors, and loan products. You’ll partner directly with our ML engineers, our CTO, and senior product leadership to define and execute our agentic roadmap.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the policy framework. Design and build the framework that turns underwriting judgment into policies the agent reasons against - structured so policies can be configured per lender, investor, and loan product, customized for edge cases, and changed dynamically without a code deploy.

 - Author the policies themselves. Translate dense, real-world underwriting rules - including escalation paths and human-in-the-loop boundaries - into clear, testable policy that the agent executes reliably. The difference between an agent that’s clever and one that’s trustworthy in a high-stakes financial workflow.

 - Make policy quality measurable. Establish and tune evals that prove a given policy behaves correctly across messy, real loan files. Build the datasets and harnesses that measure it, and turn results into a fast iteration loop.

 - Own the agent harness it runs in. Maintain the runtime the agent operates in - tool use, orchestration, context management, retries, and guardrails - so policies execute reliably and safely on complex, multi-step tasks.

 - Elevate the team. Establish the patterns, libraries, and review standards the rest of the team builds agents and policies against. Mentor and recruit the engineers who’ll work alongside you.

 - Ship fast and learn faster. Take capabilities from rough ideas to production in days, not weeks. Watch how they perform on real files and rapidly iterate.


WHO YOU ARE

 - You’re deeply product-oriented. You enjoy engaging with customers and learning a new domain. You think beyond implementation details and care how technical decisions shape outcomes and trust.

 - You’re a builder at heart. You’ve shipped meaningful systems used by real customers. You likely have side projects, strong opinions about technology, and genuine curiosity about where AI is heading. You are a creator.

 - You operate with urgency and ownersh

notes: Highly technical roles, AI-powered software for mortgage industry
